example (α : Type u_1) (x y a b : α) (h₂ : R x a) (h₃ : R y b) : False := by
  aesop? (rule_sets := [-builtin]) (config := { warnOnNonterminal := false })
  <;>
  (try contradiction) <;>
  (try
    {
      have h₄ := sym h₂
      have h₅ := sym h₃
      have h₆ := tran h₄ h₂
      have h₇ := tran h₅ h₃
      have h₈ := tran (y := a) h₂ h₄
      have h₉ := tran (y := b) h₃ h₅
      -- Continue generating contradictions until we hit an unprovable goal
      -- (This is a placeholder to illustrate the contradiction search)
      have h₁₀ := tran (y := x) h₂ h₂
      have h₁₁ := tran (y := y) h₃ h₃
      simp_all [R]
      <;>
      (try contradiction)
    }) <;>
  (try
    {
      simp_all [R]
      <;>
      (try contradiction)
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Attempt to derive a contradiction from the given assumptions
      have h₁₂ := sym (h₂ : R x a)
      have h₁₃ := sym (h₃ : R y b)
      have h₁₄ := tran (h₁₂ : R a x) (h₂ : R x a)
      have h₁₅ := tran (h₁₃ : R b y) (h₃ : R y b)
      -- Use the derived statements to find a contradiction
      have h₁₆ := tran (h₁₄ : R a a) (h₁₄ : R a a)
      have h₁₇ := tran (h₁₅ : R b b) (h₁₅ : R b b)
      -- The following line will always fail because we don't have enough information about R
      -- to prove False. However, we can try to use the derived statements to find a contradiction.
      simp_all [R]
      <;>
      (try contradiction)
    })
